name: Q1. Footway Presence
definition: Is a dedicated pedestrian footway (sidewalk) present along this street segment?
evidence: Visible sidewalk structures (concrete, paving, asphalt) separating pedestrians from vehicular lanes.
positiveExamples: Paved concrete sidewalk adjacent to building fronts
Asphalt footpath running parallel to road
negativeExamples: Pedestrians walking directly on the asphalt roadway with no path marker
Dirt road shoulder with no pedestrian space
unknownRule: Rate as Cannot Determine if the view of the segment is completely blocked by large vehicles or extreme flare.

name: Q2. Footway Condition
definition: What is the overall condition of the pedestrian footway?
positiveExamples: Smooth brick paving or new concrete
No visible trip hazards or broken panels
negativeExamples: Cracked concrete, grass growing from large gaps, uneven surface
unknownRule: Select Cannot Determine if camera angle prevents direct view of pavement condition.

name: Q3. Footway Obstructions
definition: To what extent is the footway obstructed by permanent or semi-permanent objects (e.g., parked vehicles, vendors, utility poles, debris)?
evidence: Physical obstacles in the walking space.
positiveExamples: Completely clear pathway with zero obstructions
Trees placed off to the side with plenty of clearance
negativeExamples: Sidewalk blocked by vendor stalls or parked vehicles forcing detour
unknownRule: Select Cannot Determine if visual blocks obscure the sidewalk view.

name: Q4. Encroachment
definition: To what extent is the pedestrian footway encroached upon by permanent, semi-permanent, or temporary objects (e.g., vendor stalls, parked vehicles, construction debris)?
evidence: Visible blockages or narrowing of the pedestrian clear zone.
positiveExamples: No vendors, garbage, or vehicles parked on the sidewalk; walk path is fully clear (None).
negativeExamples: Multiple parked motorbikes and garbage piles occupying 50%+ of the footway width (Major).
unknownRule: Select Cannot Determine only if the footway area is completely hidden.

name: Q5. Street Lighting
evidence: Visible lampposts, poles with light fixtures, or wall-mounted lighting units.
positiveExamples: Tall metal overhead light poles running along the segment
negativeExamples: Zero visible light fixtures or overhead light infrastructure
unknownRule: Select Cannot Determine if details cannot be resolved due to low light or distance.

name: Q6. Greenery
definition: How much street greenery is visible?
evidence: Presence of street trees, planter boxes, shrubs, lawns, or overhead tree cover.
positiveExamples: Dense tree cover or continuous line of street trees
negativeExamples: Completely paved urban area with no soil, grass, or plants
unknownRule: Select Cannot Determine if vegetation blocks camera or segment details are unseen.

name: Q7. Overall Walkability
definition: Considering all images together, rate the overall walkability of this street segment.
evidence: Holistic evaluation of safety, path quality, width, obstructions, and comfort.
positiveExamples: Wide, clean, safe, obstacle-free footway with greenery
negativeExamples: No footway, high speed vehicle traffic, major obstacles, debris
unknownRule: Do not mark Cannot Determine unless images are unreadable.

name: Q8. Safety
definition: Rate the pedestrian safety of this street segment.
evidence: Visual indicators of safety from traffic hazards or physical design.
positiveExamples: Physical crash barriers separating path from traffic, slow vehicle speeds
negativeExamples: High-speed traffic lanes directly adjacent to footway with no barriers
unknownRule: Use Cannot Determine if view of the traffic environment is blocked.

name: Q9. Comfort
definition: Rate the pedestrian comfort of this street segment.
evidence: Shade, walking path width, surface flatness, and ease of walking.
positiveExamples: Flat smooth surface, cooling tree shade, wide walking area
negativeExamples: Potholes, direct sun heat, noise, narrow crowded path
unknownRule: Use Cannot Determine if street layout is unclear.

name: Q10. Pleasurability
definition: Rate the pedestrian pleasurability of this street segment.
evidence: Aesthetics, presence of active storefronts, clean surroundings, greenery.
positiveExamples: Clean, lively shopping street with greenery and attractive facades
negativeExamples: Littered, sterile, high-pollution street bordered by blank concrete walls
unknownRule: Use Cannot Determine if general ambiance is unresolvable.

name: Q11. Which pedestrian-related problems are visible?
evidence: Presence of specific pedestrian infrastructure issues or environmental hazards.

name: Q12. How clearly could you assess this street segment?
evidence: Camera visibility, presence of glare, shadows, dirt on lens, or obstructions.
positiveExamples: Bright daylight, clear street view with no glare or blockages
negativeExamples: Dense tree branches right in front of camera, or dirty lens

name: Q13. Additional Comments (Optional)
definition: Provide any observations that influenced your ratings but were not captured above.
